theme: The Office Has Memory — Moments That Leave a Mark

ideas.title: Standup Circle — Meeting Mode
ideas.description: Add a 'Standup' trigger (manual button or time-based, e.g. 9:55 AM) that temporarily pulls all worker avatars off their desks and arranges them in a small circle in the center of the office floor. Each avatar faces inward. A 'talking stick' (tiny microphone emoji) rotates clockwise around the circle every 8 seconds. After 90 seconds (or manual dismiss), everyone returns to their desks with a slide animation. Build as a separate office layout mode passed as a prop: officeMode: 'standup' | 'work'. WHY: Standups are the ritual heartbeat of any dev team. Visualizing them makes the dashboard feel like it tracks team rhythms, not just task throughput. It also creates an obvious, shareable moment — a screenshot of your team in standup circle is immediately recognizable and fun.

ideas.title: Growing Office Plant Tracks Vibe Streak
ideas.description: Place a small potted plant (pure CSS, using border-radius and gradients — no images) in the bottom corner of the office floor. The plant has 5 growth stages rendered as CSS classes: seedling → sprout → small plant → leafy → blooming. It advances one stage for every 5-unit increase in vibe streak (using the existing vibeStreak state). If the streak resets to 0, the plant wilts (drooping leaves CSS transform) and slowly recovers over 60 seconds rather than snapping back. On hover: 'Fern the office ficus. Growing with your momentum.' WHY: Streaks are abstract numbers. A plant externalizes the streak as something organic and alive — you feel the loss of a reset more when you see the plant droop. It also rewards consistent performance with a visible flourishing office environment.

ideas.title: High-Five Spark Between Back-to-Back Completions
ideas.description: When two different workers both complete a task within 30 seconds of each other, render a brief SVG lightning arc (a curved, animated stroke) between their two desk positions on the office floor. The arc appears over 300ms, glows for 500ms, then fades. The color is a blend of both workers' role colors. Track last-completion timestamp per worker in a ref; on each new completion, check if any other worker completed within the 30s window. WHY: Simultaneous shipping is a team moment, not two individual moments. A visual connection between workers who are 'in sync' creates the feeling of a collaborative flow state that text logs completely miss. It's also a subtle, beautiful reward for coordinated work.

ideas.title: Error Weather: Rain on the Office Window
ideas.description: The existing day/night window background is static. Wire it to recent error rate: when errors per minute exceed a threshold (e.g. 2+ errors in 5 minutes), CSS-animated rain drops appear on the window (vertical lines falling at varying speeds and opacities, pure CSS). Heavy error rate = heavy rain. Zero errors for 5 minutes = rain fades and sun or stars reappear. Implement as a separate WeatherLayer component that reads a derived errorIntensity prop (0–1 scale). WHY: Error spikes are anxiety-inducing. Externalizing that anxiety as weather is psychologically smart — it acknowledges the storm without alarming anyone, and it provides an ambient signal that something needs attention without a red alert. When the rain clears, the relief is palpable.

ideas.title: Ghost Desk for Long-Idle Workers
ideas.description: When a worker slot has had no activity (no task start, no completion, no error) for more than 15 minutes, replace their character sprite with a semi-transparent ghostly silhouette at 25% opacity with a slow float animation (translateY ±4px, 4s ease-in-out). A small tooltip on hover: 'Last seen X minutes ago.' When they become active again, the ghost dissolves in a ripple effect and the full-color character reappears. WHY: Right now a long-idle worker looks exactly like a worker mid-task. Ghost desks communicate absence without a disruptive alert — it's a compassionate, ambient signal that lets the team notice without calling anyone out.

ideas.title: Session XP Badges on Worker Cards
ideas.description: Track a per-session completion count per worker in a sessionStorage map (reset on page reload). Display a small badge pill on each worker card: '✦ 3', '✦ 10', etc. At milestone counts (5, 10, 25, 50), the badge briefly animates (scale bounce + color flash in the worker's role color) and the milestone number is bold. Badge is hidden at 0. Persist only for the current browser session — this is a 'today's score', not a permanent leaderboard. WHY: Task completions are the atomic unit of team output but they vanish into the log. A live session badge makes each worker's contribution visible at a glance, creates friendly competition, and gives a satisfying sense of accumulation during long sessions.

ideas.title: Code Red — Multi-Error Panic Mode
ideas.description: When 3 or more workers hit errors within a 60-second window, trigger a 10-second 'Code Red' state: the office floor background pulses with a very subtle red tint (max opacity 0.08, 3 slow pulses), all character avatars display a tiny 🚨 badge, and a banner appears at the top: 'The team is in the weeds. Ship something.' The state auto-dismisses after 10 seconds or when any worker completes a task, whichever comes first. Dismissal triggers a brief green flash. Respect the existing reducedMotion flag — in reduced motion mode, just show the banner with no pulses. WHY: Coordinated failure is qualitatively different from individual failure. When the whole team is erroring at once, something systemic is wrong — a bad deploy, a flaky dependency, a blocked queue. Code Red names that moment explicitly and creates urgency without a Slack ping. The 'ship something' prompt is a gentle, gamified nudge toward resolution.
